skill.name: risk-management-specialist
skill.description: Medical device risk management guidance for ISO 14971 workflows, including risk analysis, risk evaluation, risk controls, verification planning, and risk management file maintenance.
skill.summary: Guide ISO 14971 risk management for medical device lifecycle work.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed all static weak cryptography and Ruby/shell backtick alerts as false positives. The lines contain Markdown formatting, filenames, placeholder reference text, and medical-device risk-management guidance. No command execution, weak crypto implementation, exfiltration, or prompt injection evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported lines contain natural-language medical device, design, safety, error, and authentication references, not cryptographic code. Duplicate static hits at SKILL.md lines 3, 172, and 183 were the same benign text context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations show prose and placeholder documentation only. I found no hashing, encryption, key handling, or algorithm implementation at the reported lines.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Ruby or Shell Backtick Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported backticks are Markdown code fences and inline resource filenames. They do not invoke Ruby, shell command substitution, external binaries, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are formatting delimiters or quoted filenames in documentation. No executable shell, Ruby, or command construction syntax is present.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams need consistent ISO 14971 risk work. This skill guides risk analysis, controls, verification, and file maintenance.
actual_capabilities: Build ISO 14971 risk management plans and file outlines.
Identify hazards, hazardous situations, harms, and foreseeable misuse.
Evaluate probability, severity, residual risk, and acceptability criteria.
Define risk controls and verification evidence for medical devices.
Connect software, cybersecurity, human factors, and post-market risk activities.
limitations: Does not replace a qualified regulatory or clinical reviewer.
Needs device-specific data, intended use, and risk acceptability criteria.
Cannot certify compliance or guarantee regulator acceptance.
Placeholder assets and scripts do not provide automated tooling.

use_cases.title: Plan Risk Management Work
use_cases.description: Create a structured ISO 14971 plan with scope, roles, criteria, and file contents.

use_cases.title: Review Device Hazards
use_cases.description: Map hazards, hazardous situations, harms, severity, probability, and initial risk ratings.

use_cases.title: Prepare Audit Evidence
use_cases.description: Organize controls, verification records, residual risk decisions, and post-market update needs.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Risk Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ISO 14971 risk management plan outline for a medical device. Include scope, roles, criteria, records, and review points.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Hazards
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for early risk analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this device description for hazards, foreseeable misuse, hazardous situations, possible harms, and missing information needed for risk estimation.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Risk Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these risk controls against ISO 14971 hierarchy. Identify verification evidence, residual risk decisions, and gaps in effectiveness evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Update the Risk File
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this post-market signal or design change. Explain risk file updates, affected hazards, control changes, benefit-risk impact, and documentation actions.

output_examples.input: A team asks for a risk analysis structure for a connected infusion pump.
output_examples.output: A structured risk analysis covering intended use, misuse, cybersecurity hazards, software failure modes, harms, controls, and evidence gaps.

output_examples.input: A reviewer provides risk controls and asks what verification is missing.
output_examples.output: A control review that separates design controls, protective measures, labeling, verification evidence, and residual risk decisions.

output_examples.input: A post-market complaint suggests a new use error pattern.
output_examples.output: An update plan for affected hazards, probability assumptions, human factors controls, labeling, and risk management file records.

best_practices: Provide intended use, user groups, clinical context, and device architecture before requesting risk analysis.
Define severity, probability, and acceptability criteria before asking for risk ratings.
Ask for traceable outputs that connect hazards, controls, verification, and residual risk decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use generic hazards without connecting them to device-specific hazardous situations.
Do not treat suggested controls as approved design decisions without verification evidence.
Do not ask for final regulatory conclusions when clinical and post-market evidence is missing.

faq.question: Does this skill implement ISO 14971?
faq.answer: It provides structured guidance for ISO 14971 activities. Your organization remains responsible for documented procedures and approvals.

faq.question: Can it create a complete risk management file?
faq.answer: It can outline and draft file content. It needs device data, criteria, test evidence, and reviewer decisions.

faq.question: Does it cover software risk?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes IEC 62304 software risk integration and software contribution to hazardous situations.

faq.question: Does it address cybersecurity risk?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports medical device cybersecurity threat modeling, controls, and patient safety impact review.

faq.question: Can it replace regulatory consultants?
faq.answer: No. It supports drafting and review work, but qualified experts must approve regulated deliverables.

faq.question: What inputs improve results?
faq.answer: Provide intended use, device description, architecture, users, hazards, complaints, controls, and current risk criteria.
